{{Infobox ancient site | name = Minamikata Kofun cluster | native_name =南方古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Minamikata Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nobeoka, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of burial mounds is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[Sue ware]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Nobeoka Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1852 Cultural Heritage of Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nobeoka.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/62/3485.html Nobeoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[Sue ware]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Nobeoka Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[Sue ware]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Nobeoka Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Minamikata Kofun cluster | native_name =南方古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Minamikata Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nobeoka, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of burial mounds is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[Sue ware]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Nobeoka Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1852 Cultural Heritage of Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nobeoka.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/62/3485.html Nobeoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of burial mounds is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[Sue ware]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Nobeoka Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Nobeoka Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Nobeoka Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Nobeoka Domain|Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[:en:Stone tool|Stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Nobeoka Station|Nobeoka Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Nobeoka Domain|Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[:en:Stone tool|Stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Nobeoka Station|Nobeoka Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1852 Cultural Heritage of Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nobeoka.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/62/3485.html Nobeoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>

The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Nobeoka Domain|Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[:en:Stone tool|Stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Nobeoka Station|Nobeoka Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1852 Cultural Heritage of Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nobeoka.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/62/3485.html Nobeoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Minamikata Kofun cluster | native_name =南方古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Minamikata Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki|Nobeoka, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Nobeoka Domain|Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[:en:Stone tool|Stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Nobeoka Station|Nobeoka Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1852 Cultural Heritage of Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nobeoka.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/62/3485.html Nobeoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Nobeoka Domain|Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[:en:Stone tool|Stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Nobeoka Station|Nobeoka Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Nobeoka Domain|Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[:en:Stone tool|Stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Nobeoka Station|Nobeoka Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Minamikata Kofun cluster | native_name =南方古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Minamikata Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki|Nobeoka, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Nobeoka Domain|Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[:en:Stone tool|Stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Nobeoka Station|Nobeoka Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Minamikata Kofun cluster | native_name =南方古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Minamikata Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki|Nobeoka, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Nobeoka Domain|Nobeoka Domain]]. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous [[:en:Stone tool|Stone tool]]s, beads, gold rings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Nobeoka Station|Nobeoka Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1852 Cultural Heritage of Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nobeoka.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/62/3485.html Nobeoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Nobeoka Domain|en|Nobeoka Domain|WD=Q10863441|lt=Nobeoka Domain}}. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous {{ill|Stone tool|en|Stone tool|WD=Q479257|lt=Stone tool}}s, beads, gold rings, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Nobeoka Station|en|Nobeoka Station|WD=Q5357994|lt=Nobeoka Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Minamikata Kofun cluster | native_name =南方古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Minamikata Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki|Nobeoka, Miyazaki]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Nobeoka Domain|en|Nobeoka Domain|WD=Q10863441|lt=Nobeoka Domain}}. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous {{ill|Stone tool|en|Stone tool|WD=Q479257|lt=Stone tool}}s, beads, gold rings, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Nobeoka Station|en|Nobeoka Station|WD=Q5357994|lt=Nobeoka Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1852 Cultural Heritage of Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nobeoka.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/62/3485.html Nobeoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Nobeoka Domain|en|Nobeoka Domain|WD=Q10863441|lt=Nobeoka Domain|1=Nobeoka Domain}}. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous {{ill|Stone tool|en|Stone tool|WD=Q479257|lt=Stone tool|1=Stone tool}}s, beads, gold rings, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Nobeoka Station|en|Nobeoka Station|WD=Q5357994|lt=Nobeoka Station|1=Nobeoka Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Minamikata Kofun cluster | native_name =南方古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Minamikata Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki|Nobeoka, Miyazaki]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Nobeoka Domain|en|Nobeoka Domain|WD=Q10863441|lt=Nobeoka Domain|1=Nobeoka Domain}}. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous {{ill|Stone tool|en|Stone tool|WD=Q479257|lt=Stone tool|1=Stone tool}}s, beads, gold rings, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Nobeoka Station|en|Nobeoka Station|WD=Q5357994|lt=Nobeoka Station|1=Nobeoka Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamikata Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Minamikata Kofun cluster | native_name =南方古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Minamikata Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki|Nobeoka, Miyazaki]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|34|22|N|131|37|49|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Minamikata Kofun cluster'''|南方古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Amori-cho, Yoshino-cho, Onuki-cho, Maino-cho, Noji-cho neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Nobeoka, Miyazaki| Nobeoka]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203116|title= 南方古墳群|trans-title= Minamikata Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Minamikata Kofun Cluster consist of tumuli distributed in the low hills that open to the west of Nobeoka city in the lower reaches of the Gokase River. It has been classified into the Tenka, Onuki, Maino, Yoshino, Noji, and Noda subgroups depending on location. The tumuli date to the early 5th to 6th century, with a variety of burial facilities such as clay coffins, boat-shaped sarcophagi, and side-pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. Minamikata Kofun No. 10 in the Tenkashi group is an "ekagami-style" variation of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 78 meters and a diameter of approximately 52 meters at the posterior circular portion. The burial chamber contained a clay coffin covering a wooden coffin, and items such as ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, bamboo combs, and iron arrowheads and iron swords were found as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The history of research into this group of Kofuns is long, and it is said that it began in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} with the investigation of the Minamikata Kofun No. 24 by Naito Masayasu, the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Nobeoka Domain|en|Nobeoka Domain|qid=Q10863441|lt=Nobeoka Domain|1=Nobeoka Domain}}. In the early 20th century, surface collections were carried out around Minamikata Kofun No. 11, No. 12, and No. 13 and numerous {{ill|Stone tool|en|Stone tool|qid=Q479257|lt=Stone tool|1=Stone tool}}s, beads, gold rings, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery was found.
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Nobeoka Station|en|Nobeoka Station|qid=Q5357994|lt=Nobeoka Station|1=Nobeoka Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|qid=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1852 Cultural Heritage of Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nobeoka.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/62/3485.html Nobeoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

At the time of the National Historic Site designation, there were 42 ''kofun'', including five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but despite the protected status there are 38 ''kofun'' remaining in total. These consist of the aforementioned five ''zenpō-kōen-fun'', {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, and one horizontal corridor-type cave-tomb. On the hill facing south is the Minamikata Kofun No. 1, also an "ekagami-style" tomb with a total length of 71 meters and a diameter of 35 meter at the posterior circular portion, but it has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] because it is site of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} (Tenka Jinja) whose ''[[Honden]]'' is located above the burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
